cross_domain_flows.name: Complete Order-to-Payment Flow

steps.action: Waiter selects table from Table Map
Waiter browses available menu items
Waiter submits order with selected items
Chef views incoming order on Kitchen Display
Chef starts cooking the order
Chef views recipe guide if needed
Chef marks order as ready
Waiter sees kitchen ready notification
Waiter confirms served (acknowledges notification)
Waiter opens payment screen, inputs amount
Waiter processes payment

rollback_on_cancel: Stock deductions reversed via reversal StockMovement records (E-009)
Table.status -> available if no other active orders (E-010)
No payment impact (cancel is before payment)

timing_resolution.rationale: F-004 states 'when order is confirmed'. Deducting at submission prevents overselling. Reversal StockMovement entries created on cancel/void.

steps.action: Waiter selects menu items and taps Submit Order
side_effects: System checks stock against recipe ingredients (RecipeIngredient E-006)

steps.action: System detects insufficient stock and returns warning
side_effects: Warning dialog shown with insufficient items
Waiter can proceed or modify order

cross_domain_flows.name: Full Cashier Journey: Prep to Close Shift

steps.action: Cashier logs in
Cashier does pre-shift preparation (check menu, stock)
Cashier verifies PIN for shift opening
Cashier opens shift with opening balance
During shift: process orders and payments
side_effects: All transactions tracked under active shift (E-014)

steps.action: Cashier reviews shift summary for closing
Cashier enters actual cash and closes shift
Chef marks order as ready ('Siap Diantar')
side_effects: KitchenNotification created (E-017) with orderId, tableNumber, items

steps.action: Waiter sees notification on Order List screen
Waiter confirms served ('Sudah Diantar')
Cashier finds paid order and opens transaction detail
Cashier requests PIN verification for void
Cashier selects void type, reason, and confirms void

store_service_map.note: Reporting reads from transactions table (no dedicated entity). DailySummary/RevenueData are computed views, not stored entities.
Sync domain is the infrastructure layer itself. Push/pull endpoints coordinate data between Dexie (client) and PostgreSQL (server).

timing_resolutions.conflict_description: F-004 says 'when order is confirmed' vs UF-001 step 11 implies 'at payment completion'. All three decision layers independently chose order submission timing.
timing_resolutions.rationale: Deducting at submission prevents overselling during cooking. Cancelled/voided orders create reversal StockMovement entries to restore stock. This is the safer approach for UMKM restaurants with limited inventory.

per_feature.title: Digital Order Management
per_feature.notes: All fields (table_number, menu_items, special_notes, order_status, is_additional) mapped. All 8 business rules mapped to entity constraints and endpoint rules.

per_feature.title: Kitchen Display System
per_feature.notes: Kitchen queue, status transitions (pending->cooking->ready->served), elapsed time, late order highlighting, notification flow all covered.

per_feature.title: Cashier & Payment Processing
per_feature.notes: All payment methods (tunai, qris, gopay, ovo, dana, shopeepay, transfer_bank) mapped. PPN calculation, discount before PPN, receipt printing, success popup all covered.

per_feature.title: Inventory / Stock Management
per_feature.notes: Auto-deduction on order, restock, manual adjustment, stock movement history, threshold alerts all covered.

per_feature.title: Menu Management
per_feature.notes: Full CRUD for menu items. Availability toggle, price changes, image upload, category assignment all covered.

per_feature.title: Recipe Guide / Cooking Instructions
per_feature.notes: Ingredients linked to inventory, step-by-step instructions, viewable from Kitchen Display. Chef edit permission covered.

per_feature.title: Daily Sales Report & Dashboard
per_feature.notes: Dashboard with revenue, order count, top items, payment breakdown, revenue by hour. Date range filtering. Real-time updates.

per_feature.title: Checklist-based Access Control
per_feature.notes: Permission checklist per user. Owner-only access. Immediate effect. Core permission protection. Default role permissions with overrides.

per_feature.title: Multi-role Authentication / Login
per_feature.notes: Local-first auth with bcrypt. PIN management. Role-based routing to Dashboard Hub. User CRUD by owner. Failed login logging.

per_feature.title: Cash Register Shift Management
per_feature.notes: PIN verification, opening balance, one-way closing, cash reconciliation, mandatory notes for discrepancy, immutable closed shifts, shift history for owner.

per_feature.title: POS System Settings (Tax/PPN)
per_feature.notes: PPN enable/disable, rate configuration, restaurant info, receipt customization. Non-retroactive changes.

per_feature.title: Void / Cancel Order & Transaction
per_feature.notes: PIN verification for void. Full/partial void. Mandatory reason. Cash impact on shift. Immutable audit trail. Cancel before payment supported.

per_feature.title: Table Management

rules_inferred.rule: Table count is configurable by owner via EP-042 (create) and EP-044 (delete)

per_feature.notes: Visual grid with status, tap to create/view order, auto-status updates. Owner configures table count.

per_feature.title: Transaction History

rules_inferred.rule: Transaction list supports combined filters (date, ID, PIC, shift, method, total range) with reset
Summary stats (count, total revenue, cash total, digital total) update with active filters

per_feature.notes: Advanced filtering, detail modal with PPN breakdown, Print Struk button, non-deletable by non-owners.

per_feature.title: Low Stock Notification

rules_inferred.rule: Dashboard shows lowStockAlertCount badge via EP-062 response

per_feature.notes: EP-033 returns low stock items. EP-062 dashboard includes lowStockAlertCount. Inventory table highlights low/critical badges.

per_feature.title: Discount / Promo Management
per_feature.notes: Full discount CRUD. Banner display on order screen. One discount per order. Discount before PPN. Tracked in reports.

per_feature.title: Report Export to Excel

rules_inferred.rule: Client-side Excel generation using SheetJS (xlsx package) from EP-054 data endpoint

per_feature.notes: EP-054 provides structured data endpoint. Actual .xlsx generation is client-side via SheetJS (in tech-stack). Respects active filters.

per_feature.title: Menu Categorization
per_feature.notes: Full CRUD for categories. Menu items filtered by category. Sort order supported.

check_01_entity_api_mapping.details: All 17 non-junction entities have at least one endpoint with matching related_entity. All 74 endpoints reference valid entity names and E-xxx IDs. Junction entities (UserPermission E-002, RecipeIngredient E-006) are correctly accessed via parent entity endpoints.
check_01_entity_api_mapping.note: UserPermission accessed via EP-009/EP-010 (related_entity_id: E-002). RecipeIngredient accessed via EP-022/EP-023 as nested array in recipe body.

check_02_tech_architecture_consistency.details: Architecture folder_structure matches tech-stack choices: React 18 in src/, Express.js in server/, Prisma in prisma/. Auth flow is local-auth-hash (bcrypt, no JWT) consistent across tech-stack.auth and architecture.auth_flow. Data flow is offline-first via Dexie (IndexedDB) matching key_signals.storage_primary='indexeddb-dexie'. Zustand stores match 10 domain groups.

check_03_feature_coverage.details: All 12 must_have features fully covered with entities and/or endpoints. No gaps in must_have coverage.

check_04_naming_consistency.details: Entity names: PascalCase verified (User, UserPermission, MenuItem, MenuCategory, Recipe, RecipeIngredient, RecipeStep, InventoryItem, StockMovement, Table, Order, OrderItem, Transaction, Shift, VoidRecord, Discount, KitchenNotification, PosSettings, EwalletProvider). Attribute names: camelCase verified (userId, permissionKey, itemName, categoryId, etc.). Endpoint paths: kebab-case verified (/api/menu-items, /api/inventory-items, /api/kitchen/orders, etc.).

check_05_decision_id_uniqueness.details: Entity decisions: D-001 to D-029 range (entities.json does not enumerate D-xxx IDs beyond D-001 inline). API decisions: D-030 to D-033 (4 decisions in api-design.json). System decisions: D-050 to D-065 (16 decisions across tech-stack.json and architecture.json). No duplicates detected across files.

check_06_endpoint_id_sequential.details: EP-001 through EP-074, 74 total endpoints. Sequential with no gaps: auth(EP-001..EP-004), user-management(EP-005..EP-010), menu(EP-011..EP-019), recipe(EP-020..EP-024), inventory(EP-025..EP-034), order-management(EP-035..EP-044), kitchen(EP-045..EP-049), payment(EP-050..EP-055), shift-management(EP-056..EP-061), reporting(EP-062..EP-063), settings(EP-064..EP-071), sync(EP-072..EP-074). No duplicates.

check_07_business_rules_completeness.details: All must_have features have business_rules mapped to entities and/or endpoints. Toggleable permissions (perm-view-stock, perm-view-menu, etc.) have permission_required fields on relevant endpoints. Key rules verified: PPN calculation formula consistent, stock deduction timing resolved, void PIN requirement enforced.

check_08_state_machine_validity.details: Order (E-011): 7 states, all reachable from initial 'pending'. Terminal states: paid, cancelled, voided. OrderItem (E-012): 4 states, all reachable. Terminal: served. Table (E-010): 3 states, all reachable, no terminal (cyclical). Transaction (E-013): 2 states, terminal: voided. Shift (E-014): 2 states, terminal: closed. InventoryItem (E-008) stockStatus: 3 states, no terminal (cyclical). All transitions reference valid source/target states.

check_09_trigger_timing_consistency.details: Stock deduction timing conflict resolved consistently: all three layers (entity D-001, API D-030, architecture D-064) agree on 'deduct at order submission'. EP-037 business_rules explicitly states 'Stock deduction triggered on order submission' with resolved_timing block. EP-039 handles reversal on cancellation.

check_10_cross_domain_flow_completeness.details: All 5 cross-domain flows (UF-001, UF-006, UF-007, UF-008, UF-010) have every step mapped to a specific EP-xxx endpoint. No flow step is missing an endpoint mapping. UF-001 has 11 steps across 4 domains. UF-007 has 7 steps across 4 domains.

check_11_store_service_boundary.details: All entities in cross-domain flows have sync strategy: 'offline-first' via Dexie primary store. Cross-domain events use store-to-store action calls (architecture.json cross_domain_events). Dexie transactions ensure atomicity. Sync to PostgreSQL is opportunistic/background.

check_12_per_feature_coverage.details: 18 features classified: 12 must_have (all fully_covered), 5 should_have (all fully_covered), 1 could_have (fully_covered). No partially_covered or gap features.

check_13_inferred_rules.details: Should/could features without explicit business_rules have sensible defaults inferred: F-010 (table count configurable), F-011 (combined filters, summary stats), F-012 (dashboard badge count), F-013 (client-side SheetJS generation). All inferred rules marked with source and confidence level in per_feature coverage.

check_14_summary_accuracy.details: api-design.json summary.total_endpoints=74 matches actual count (4+6+9+5+10+10+5+6+6+2+8+3=74). summary.by_domain matches each split file: auth:4, user-management:6, menu:9, recipe:5, inventory:10, order-management:10, kitchen:5, payment:6, shift-management:6, reporting:2, settings:8, sync:3. by_method totals: GET:33, POST:21, PATCH:14, DELETE:6 -- sum=74. All verified.

validation_notes.note: Junction entity RecipeIngredient (E-006) has no dedicated CRUD endpoints. It is managed as a nested array within Recipe endpoints (EP-022 POST body includes 'ingredients' array, EP-023 PATCH body can replace all ingredients). This is acceptable design for a junction entity.

Architecture data_flow.stores lists DailySummary and RevenueData under useReportingStore entities, but these are computed views (aggregations from Transaction data), not stored entities in entities.json. This is correct -- they are virtual entities, not persisted.

api-design.json domain_index shows entity counts per domain. These represent entities 'touched' by the domain, not unique entity counts (e.g., Order E-011 appears in both order-management and kitchen domains). This is expected for cross-domain entities.

InventoryItem.stockStatus state machine has no terminal states -- this is correct since stock levels cycle between ok/low/critical based on quantity changes. Table.status also has no terminal states (cyclical: available->occupied->needs_payment->available).

The timing conflict in _shared-context.json (F-004 'when order is confirmed' vs UF-001 step 11 'at payment completion') is resolved unanimously across all three decision layers. EP-037 includes explicit resolved_timing block documenting the decision and rejected alternatives.

Sync conflict resolution uses domain-specific strategy per D-031: last-write-wins for most entities, append-only semantics for financial records (Transaction, Shift, VoidRecord). This prevents accidental overwrite of immutable financial data.
